own_review_points: Double-close risk: FlowBase.close() closes the shared httpClient and TlsClientAuthFlow/ClientCredentialsFlow.close() also call exchanger.close() (TokenClient) which likely closes the same httpClient
@Slf4j on TlsClientAuthFlow breaks the package convention of @CustomLog (lombok slog) used by FlowBase/ClientCredentialsFlow
CLIENT_SECRET_POST path forwards tlsCertFile/keyFile/autoCertRefreshDuration into ClientCredentialsFlow; FlowBase WILL honor them if set, so misleading rather than truly inert
TokenEndpointAuthMethod.fromValue is case-insensitive + throws; no unit test exercises case-insensitivity or invalid-value throw
Race between sslRefreshScheduler.shutdownNow() and sslFactory.close() (in-flight refreshSslContext not awaited)
Eager PulsarSslFactory init in FlowBase constructor vs lazy initialize() in TlsClientAuthFlow
credentialsUrl is mapped to ClientCredentialsFlow.privateKey via toExternalForm() (human-ref point) — only used by client_secret_post
ClientCredentialsExchangeRequest gains @JsonProperty authMethod enum; not serialized to wire (form body built manually) so benign
notes: Full diff (54.9k, not truncated). Six substantive, grounded points; no factual errors. Double-close cites TokenClient.java:46 outside the diff but the claim is structurally grounded (two close paths over a shared client) — counted good not unverifiable. Dead-params point is slightly over-stated (FlowBase does consume certFile/keyFile if non-blank) but the core 'confusing surface' observation stands. Missed the human-reference note on credentialsUrl/privateKey mapping. Strong review overall.
